rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Known correct Claude Code facts — /rename is CLI-only.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:92 — `/rename build-system-style` appears in a bare fenced block after line:89 instructs the student to 'Open a new Cowork session'. Exercise is Cowork-only; §25 states 'No runtime rename API in Cowork or Desktop — CLI /rename is the only path.' The block has no rt-cli wrapper. Cowork students cannot execute /rename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Remove the /rename block or wrap it in an rt-cli div with a Cowork-runtime callout explaining the session name must be set at task-creation time; alternatively drop the rename entirely since the Session widget already names the task.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts — auto-load is filename-based, not folder-based.
Auto-load is filename-based: CLAUDE.md auto-loads; arbitrary files like styling-rules.md do not.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:89 — 'This is the break that proves `CLAUDE.md` and `styling-rules.md` are loaded from disk, not remembered from chat.' — implies styling-rules.md auto-loads. Per §6d, only CLAUDE.md is an auto-load filename; styling-rules.md loads only because CLAUDE.md instructs Claude to read it. The mechanism matters for students building their own systems.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rewrite line 89 to distinguish: 'CLAUDE.md auto-loads because it is the folder instruction file; styling-rules.md is read because CLAUDE.md tells Claude to use it — same effect, different mechanism.' This precision guards against students wondering why arbitrary .md files don't auto-load.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cowork is sandboxed to its connected working directories.
No session rename in Cowork or Desktop — CLI /rename is the only path.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:91-93 — bare fenced block containing `/rename build-system-style` with no runtime fork (no rt-cli div). Exercise is Cowork-only throughout (all **Prompt** blocks labelled *(Cowork)*). The renderer wraps /rename blocks in .rt-cli per §25 only when the author uses plain markdown — but the surrounding instruction at line:87 sets a new Cowork Session widget, giving no signal that the rename step is CLI-exclusive. Cowork users will paste /rename into Cowork chat, where it has no effect.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either (a) remove the /rename block — the Session widget at line:87 already names the session 'Build your system - styled answer', making the rename redundant; or (b) add an explicit rt-cli div and a Cowork-runtime callout ('In Cowork the task name is set when you open the task; skip this step').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Open-hook — ONLY when student input is genuinely required.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
Preamble before action — structural trigger: 4+ named sub-steps requires suppressor or body primer.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:65-83 — Phase 2 prompt has 7 action-level steps (Use audit, Use style source, From source propose styling-rules.md, Capture rules, Save styling-rules.md, Propose CLAUDE.md, Save CLAUDE.md). No 'No preamble.' suppressor in the fence and no body primer after the fence. line:125-137 — Phase 4 prompt has 5 action steps with no suppressor or primer.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a body primer immediately after the Phase 2 and Phase 4 fences: 'Claude will narrate before acting; skip past the opening sentence and look for the proposed styling-rules.md content.' Alternatively add 'No preamble.' as the last line inside each fence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strong-bold prompt label is exactly **Prompt** — no custom labels.
Source URL + type label — mandatory on every claim.

platform_claims_checked.claim: CLAUDE.md that tells Cowork to use those rules
Choose an empty local folder on your computer and open Cowork on it
This is the break that proves `CLAUDE.md` and `styling-rules.md` are loaded from disk, not remembered from chat
